a:r.a:t: Student or teacher accounts may be accessed by unauthorized users, exposing grades and personal data.
Use strong passwords, role-based access (student/teacher/admin), and secure authentication.
Reset compromised accounts, enable password recovery, and monitor login activity.
Students may receive wrong grades, causing disputes and inaccurate records.
Validate inputs, allow review before submission, and provide confirmation prompts for teachers.
Allow grade editing with change logs and admin approval for corrections.
Student records and grades may be lost due to server failure or software bugs.
Implement regular database backups and reliable hosting/server maintenance.
Restore from backup copies and maintain recovery procedures.
Users may not access grades or reports during important periods (e.g., grading deadlines).
Optimize database performance and test system scalability.
Provide maintenance notice and restore services quickly using backup servers.
Grades may be altered maliciously, affecting academic integrity.
Restrict grade-edit permissions to authorized teachers/admins only.
Audit logs can identify changes, and admins can restore original records.
